Pompano Beach Home Loans

FHA loans in Pompano Beach, Florida

Pompano Beach has been quietly revitalizing, with a refreshed beachfront and pier alongside established mainland neighborhoods. Its mix of condos and single-family homes, plus Broward County's high-cost FHA limit, makes it an accessible South Florida beach market for FHA buyers — provided you mind the condo-approval step that comes with so much of the coastal inventory.

Broward County FHA limit (1-unit)$667,000

2026 single-family

Minimum down3.5%

With a 580+ score

Property types1–4 units

Owner-occupied

What the FHA limit means in Pompano Beach

Broward County is a designated high-cost area, so its 2026 single-family FHA limit is $667,000, above the $541,287 statewide floor — meaning you can finance higher-priced Pompano Beach homes than in most of Florida. Confirm Broward County's exact limit with HUD's official FHA loan-limit lookup. Broward's higher limit helps, though luxury beachfront condos can still exceed it — most FHA buyers focus on mid-market condos and mainland homes.

Beach condos, the mainland, and FHA in Pompano

Much of Pompano's beachfront is condos, and FHA requires the building to be FHA-approved or the unit to qualify for Single-Unit Approval — older oceanfront buildings frequently aren't, so check first (see our condo guide). Mainland neighborhoods like Old Pompano and Cypress Bend offer single-family homes and townhomes that are simpler FHA purchases, and Broward's higher limit gives you room to work with.

Down payment help for Pompano Beach buyers

You don't have to cover the down payment alone. FHA allows gift funds, sellers can contribute up to 6% toward closing costs, and Pompano Beach buyers can often pair FHA with assistance from the Florida Housing Finance Corporation and Broward County's local SHIP-funded programs. Funding and eligibility change frequently, so we'll help you confirm what's currently available — see our FHA down payment assistance guide.

Neighborhoods we serve in Pompano Beach

We help buyers throughout Pompano Beach and Broward County, including Old Pompano, Cypress Bend, and the beachfront. Wherever you're looking, we'll explain your FHA loan limit, rate, and monthly payment — and get you pre-approved fast.

Questions & Answers

Frequently asked questions

What is the FHA loan limit in Pompano Beach (Broward County) for 2026?+
Broward County is a high-cost area, so its 2026 FHA limit is $667,000, above the $541,287 Florida floor. Confirm the exact figure with HUD's official lookup.
Can I buy a Pompano Beach condo with FHA?+
Yes, if the building is FHA-approved or the unit qualifies for Single-Unit Approval. Older beachfront buildings often aren't approved, so we check first.
How much down payment do I need in Pompano Beach?+
As little as 3.5% with a 580+ score; gift funds and seller credits up to 6% can help.
Are there single-family FHA options in Pompano Beach?+
Yes — mainland neighborhoods offer single-family homes and townhomes that are straightforward FHA purchases.
Do you serve all of Broward County?+
Yes — Pompano Beach, Fort Lauderdale, Deerfield Beach, and surrounding communities.
